subject: Re: MJ Experton
body:
what do you think?

----- Original Message ----
From: "DKIESQ@aol.com" <DKIESQ@aol.com>
To: jeeproject@yahoo.com
Sent: Tuesday, December 19, 2006 3:59:13 PM
Subject: MJ Experton



 


I RECEIVED THE FOLLOWING E-MAIL FROM MJ EXPERTON 
CONTAINING HER PROPOSAL FOR CONTINUED REPRESENTATION OF YOU IN CONNECTION WITH 
ADMINISTRATIVE MATTERS.  YOU MAY RECALL THAT SHE NO LONGER WISHED TO DO 
THIS WORK BECAUSE, AMONG OTHER THINGS, SHE FELT THAT IT WAS BENEATH HER STATUS 
AS A PROFESSIONAL.  SHE ASKED THAT I FIND SOMEONE ELSE FOR YOU.  
I TRIED TO HAVE YOUR NEW FRENCH TAX COUNSEL TAKE OVER THE WORK, BUT TAX 
COUNSEL'S FIRM REFUSED TO TAKE ON THAT WORK.

EXPERTON'S PROPOSAL WILL COST YOU 18,000 EUROS 
FOR ADMINISTRATIVE WORK ALONE.  HER INVOICE FROM AUGUST 2005 TO JUNE 
2006 FOR ALL MATTERS, INCLUDING WORK ON FOCHAL LITIGATION, ONLY 
AMOUNTED TO 8,372 EUROS.

HOWEVER, HER KNOWLEDGE OR OUR ISSUES AND HISTORY 
PROBABLY MAKES IT WORTH WHILE TO KEEP HER ON, ESPECIALLY WHEN WE HAVE NO ONE 
ELSE TO HANDLE THESE MATTERS FOR YOU.

WHAT DO YOU THINK?  PLEASE ADVISE AFTER 
READING BELOW.

DARREN

 

From MJ Experton 12/18/06 e-mail to 
DKI:

Further to your mail of last week and in order to get 
back to a clear situation before the end of the year, I would like to know if 
you have found someone to take over Mr. Epstein’s French administrative matters. 


 

If yes:

please provide name and telephone number as soon as 
possible, so I can take the necessary steps and make contacts on Wednesday when 
I am in Paris. 


 

If no:

I would be ready to make the following proposal: 


 

I will issue an invoice under the current conditions for 
the work done during the months of November 
and December 2006. 

 

For the work to be done as from 
January 
1st, 2007, I would issue a monthly invoice on a lump 
sum basis to cover the usual administrative follow up, expenses for one trip 
a month to Paris 
and normal costs for communication. I propose this 
monthly amount to be 1,500.00 euros for the year 2007, corresponding to 
approximately an average over the year of 4 to 5 hours per month at an averaged 
hourly rate of 300 euros. The list of administrative matters to be included in 
the « usual administrative follow-up » will include:

·                    
Maintenance contracts and invoices 
concerning Mr. Epstein’s apartment at 22 avenue Foch,

·                    
Relationship with condominium 
ownership (“copropriété”) at 22 
avenue Foch, preparation and attendance of the annual general meeting (once a 
year),

·                    
Matters relating to Valdson’s 
employment and French social charges for Mr. Epstein as employer.

 

Any extra work (i.e.: legal advise, litigation, 
translation work, etc.), not included in the usual administrative follow-up, 
would be charged on the basis of an hourly rate ranking between a minimum of 200 
euros (i.e.: for translation) and a maximum of 400 euros (i.e.: for litigation), 
depending on the level of difficulty of the work to be done. This extra work and 
any exceptional costs and disbursements (such as, for example, dispatch by 
international courier, special trip to Paris specifically for Mr. Epstein, etc…) 
would be invoiced separately. 

 

If appropriate, would you please let me know as soon as 
possible if you are in agreement with the above proposal.

 

Please note that I shall be away from 
Brussels from Friday 
December 22nd through Tuesday January 2nd included. I would 
appreciate having all this matter settled before leaving.

 

Best regards and seasonal greetings.

 

Marie-Joseph Experton

 

*************************************************************


 

Darren K. 
Indyke
Attorney-At-Law
457 Madison Avenue
4th Floor
New York, New 
York 10022
Tel: 212-750-1176
Fax: 212-750-0381
e-mail: dkiesq@aol.com
 
 
Confidentiality 
Notice: This communication and any attachments contain information from Darren 
K. Indyke, Esq. that constitute attorney work product or that are otherwise 
confidential and/or privileged. Except for personal use by the intended 
recipient, or as expressly authorized by the sender, any person who receives 
this information is prohibited from disclosing, copying, distributing, and/or 
using it. If you have received this communication in error, please immediately 
delete it and all copies, and promptly notify the sender at the above telephone 
number or electronic mail address. Nothing in this communication is intended to 
operate as an electronic signature under applicable 
law.

what do you think?

----- Original Message ----
From: "DKIESQ@aol.com" <DKIESQ@aol.com>
To: jeeproject@yahoo.com
Sent: Tuesday, December 19, 2006 3:59:13 PM
Subject: MJ Experton

I RECEIVED THE FOLLOWING E-MAIL FROM MJ EXPERTON CONTAINING HER PROPOSAL FOR CONTINUED REPRESENTATION OF YOU IN CONNECTION WITH ADMINISTRATIVE MATTERS.  YOU MAY RECALL THAT SHE NO LONGER WISHED TO DO THIS WORK BECAUSE, AMONG OTHER THINGS, SHE FELT THAT IT WAS BENEATH HER STATUS AS A PROFESSIONAL.  SHE ASKED THAT I FIND SOMEONE ELSE FOR YOU.  I TRIED TO HAVE YOUR NEW FRENCH TAX COUNSEL TAKE OVER THE WORK, BUT TAX COUNSEL'S FIRM REFUSED TO TAKE ON THAT WORK.

EXPERTON'S PROPOSAL WILL COST YOU 18,000 EUROS FOR ADMINISTRATIVE WORK ALONE.  HER INVOICE FROM AUGUST 2005 TO JUNE 2006 FOR ALL MATTERS, INCLUDING WORK ON FOCHAL LITIGATION, ONLY AMOUNTED TO 8,372 EUROS.

HOWEVER, HER KNOWLEDGE OR OUR ISSUES AND HISTORY PROBABLY MAKES IT WORTH WHILE TO KEEP HER ON, ESPECIALLY WHEN WE HAVE NO ONE ELSE TO HANDLE THESE MATTERS FOR YOU.

WHAT DO YOU THINK?  PLEASE ADVISE AFTER READING BELOW.

DARREN

 

From MJ Experton 12/18/06 e-mail to DKI:

Further to your mail of last week and in order to get back to a clear situation before the end of the year, I would like to know if you have found someone to take over Mr. Epstein’s French administrative matters.

 

If yes:

please provide name and telephone number as soon as possible, so I can take the necessary steps and make contacts on Wednesday when I am in Paris.

 

If no:

I would be ready to make the following proposal:

 

I will issue an invoice under the current conditions for the work done during the months of November and December 2006.

 

For the work to be done as from January 1st, 2007, I would issue a monthly invoice on a lump sum basis to cover the usual administrative follow up, expenses for one trip a month to Paris and normal costs for communication. I propose this monthly amount to be 1,500.00 euros for the year 2007, corresponding to approximately an average over the year of 4 to 5 hours per month at an averaged hourly rate of 300 euros. The list of administrative matters to be included in the « usual administrative follow-up » will include:

·                    Maintenance contracts and invoices concerning Mr. Epstein’s apartment at 22 avenue Foch,

·                    Relationship with condominium ownership (“copropriété”) at 22 avenue Foch, preparation and attendance of the annual general meeting (once a year),

·                    Matters relating to Valdson’s employment and French social charges for Mr. Epstein as employer.

 

Any extra work (i.e.: legal advise, litigation, translation work, etc.), not included in the usual administrative follow-up, would be charged on the basis of an hourly rate ranking between a minimum of 200 euros (i.e.: for translation) and a maximum of 400 euros (i.e.: for litigation), depending on the level of difficulty of the work to be done. This extra work and any exceptional costs and disbursements (such as, for example, dispatch by international courier, special trip to Paris specifically for Mr. Epstein, etc…) would be invoiced separately.

 

If appropriate, would you please let me know as soon as possible if you are in agreement with the above proposal.

 

Please note that I shall be away from Brussels from Friday December 22nd through Tuesday January 2nd included. I would appreciate having all this matter settled before leaving.

 

Best regards and seasonal greetings.

 

Marie-Joseph Experton
